title: Routing and Single-Page Applications

learningObjectives.text: Apply client-side routing using a new example and justify the result.
Explain routes and navigation using a new example and justify the result.
Explain spa mental model using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: This topic turns routing and single-page applications from isolated terminology into a practical tool for reading, writing, and reviewing code. It belongs to the **Complex State, Routing, and Styling** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Client-side routing** → **Routes and navigation** → **SPA mental model**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Client-side routing
sections.body: Client-side routing maps URLs to UI without requesting a full document for every navigation.

sections.title: Routes and navigation
sections.body: Routes represent user-visible resources and tasks. Use links for navigation and parameters for identity while preserving browser history.

sections.title: SPA mental model
sections.body: A single-page application updates views on the client after an application shell loads, requiring deliberate loading, errors, metadata, and accessibility.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: Routes map URLs to UI and a parameter identifies a track.

commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.
summary: Routing and Single-Page Applications connects Client-side routing, Routes and navigation, SPA mental model.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

choices.label: Anchors cannot navigate to relative paths.

contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.

assessmentProfile.label: Mental model plus practical code reasoning
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic combines an important model with implementation consequences, so both explanation and code reasoning are required.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
